page: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized fifth-generation server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware included, and the baseline network bandwidth is 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the r5b.large exhibits modest processing and memory throughput capabilities. In stress-ng testing, single-core performance is categorized as weak (bottom 25%), while multi-core performance falls into the poor category (bottom 10%). Geekbench testing shows an average single-core score of 1138.0, but the multi-core score of 1356.0 is poor. Memory bandwidth benchmarks also register in the bottom 10%, with read-only large block speeds at 12.97 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.36 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are similarly positioned in the bottom 10% tier.

The primary tradeoff of the r5b.large is its high memory allocation relative to its limited compute capacity and lower memory bandwidth. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a low-cost entry point into the memory-optimized class, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without the expense of larger multi-core instances. However, the low CPU and memory throughput benchmarks suggest it is not suited for compute-intensive tasks or high-throughput database workloads. It is best utilized for small-scale memory-bound workloads, lightweight caching, or development environments requiring a larger memory footprint than standard general-purpose instances.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU resources, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with stress-ng multi-core and memory bandwidth results falling into the bottom 10% tier, while Geekbench single-core performance is average. This instance represents a cost-effective entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high compute power. It is suited for small-scale caching, lightweight database operations, and memory-bound development environments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.1 GHz processor, designed for small-scale memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight, memory-bound workloads requiring dedicated x86_64 processing.

16.0 GB of DDR4 memory with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Built on the Nitro hypervisor with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for lightweight caching and small memory-bound applications
